headline: Hasbro (HAS) Leans On Digital Gaming Growth While Valuation Still Looks Compelling
summary: Hasbro (HAS) has drawn attention after launching Blooms by Play Doh, an adult oriented crafting line that extends the Play Doh brand into home décor, with price points starting around $24.99. See our latest analysis for Hasbro. Hasbro’s recent product and licensing announcements, including Blooms by Play Doh and the upcoming Legend of Zelda collectibles, arrive as the stock trades at $82.09, with a 7 day share price return of 6.07% but a 90 day share price return that has fallen 15.50%. headline: Does Hasbro’s (HAS) Zelda Toy Deal Reveal a Deeper Shift Toward Premium IP Partnerships?
summary: Earlier this month, Nintendo announced a multi-year licensing agreement with Hasbro to create The Legend of Zelda toys from 2027, including six-inch action figures debuting at San Diego Comic-Con 2026, aligned with the planned 2027 live-action film. This move extends Hasbro’s reach deeper into premium, story-driven collectibles, reinforcing its role as an IP-focused partner for major entertainment franchises. headline: Hasbro (HAS) Stock Fair Value Edges Lower As Analysts Weigh Brand Strength And Caution
summary: Hasbro’s fair value estimate has been trimmed from US$113.07 to US$109.93, signaling a more cautious stance on where analysts see the stock settling in their models. That shift aligns with research that balances optimism around core brands and earnings power with more restrained valuation assumptions and a choppier near term setup. As you read on, you will see how this evolving price target story fits into the wider Hasbro narrative and what to watch as new information comes through. Analyst...
